notes: Re-scoped from the old us_china_supply_chains, whose name ('Strategic supply chain competition and dependencies') was a category, not a phenomenon. The real 2026 content is critical minerals as CHINA'S counter-leverage to US export controls: the rare-earth truce, China placing two US rare-earth producers on its own export-control list, the $12bn US stockpile, Pentagon 'deal team six', gallium/magnets.
Measured inside the dyad gate, all 0 Iran: rare earth 60, critical mineral 37, supply chain 23, stockpile 9, magnet 4, MP Materials 2, gallium 1.
DROPPED 'refinery'/'refining' -- direct collision with the Iran oil story (US sanctions on Chinese 'teapot' refineries), which is the dominant leak class in this dyad.

The mineral sub-vocabulary is genuinely thin in the corpus; 'rare earth' + 'critical mineral' carry the atomic. Low-volume specifics (neodymium, dysprosium, germanium, graphite) are kept as cheap, zero-risk fixed nouns for future coverage.
